summary: A close review of the 2026-04-21 absorption refresh pass found duplicated refresh blocks, registry-first intake source stems, and a promotion template misaligned with older mature syntheses. The corpus has now been reset again so the 180 intake sources use human-curated English summary stems, and the workflow now has a reusable slug-manifest helper plus lint/preflight enforcement against registry-first or noncanonical source stems.

observations.problem: The refresh_existing_synthesis pass added a new update layer in three places at once: a Short answer note, an Evidence considered sub-block, and an Analysis sub-block headed `2026-04-21 흡수 갱신`. This keeps structural validation green, but it makes mature synthesis pages read like stacked batch artifacts instead of coherent synthesis pages.
observations.why_it_matters: Readers now have to parse the same refresh event multiple times, and mature pages lose the clean distinction between durable synthesis prose and later intake bookkeeping.
observations.automation_candidate: Define one canonical refresh presentation contract for existing synthesis pages. The refresh workflow should place absorbed-source updates in a single bounded location, summarize the delta once, and avoid duplicating the same refresh marker across Short answer, Evidence considered, and Analysis.
observations.suggested_followup: Updated on 2026-04-22 KST: `ops/scripts/raw_intake_promotion_runtime.py` plus `ops/scripts/raw_intake_promotion.py` now scaffold refresh profiles directly from the absorption matrix and existing synthesis pages, reviewed manifests render refreshed synthesis pages with merged evidence lists and integrated analysis prose, and `wiki_lint` now carries `synthesis_follow_up_split_candidate` as a closeout backstop against dated split refresh blocks.

observations.surface: wiki/source--*-2026-04-21.md, wiki/synthesis--*-2026-04-12.md, wiki/synthesis--*-2026-04-13.md, wiki/synthesis--*-2026-04-14.md, wiki/synthesis--*-2026-04-18.md, wiki/synthesis--*-2026-04-22.md, system/system-raw-registry/wiki/*-2026-04-21.md, ops/raw-registry.json, ops/scripts/source_slug_curation.py, ops/scripts/source_page_naming_runtime.py
observations.problem: The intake workflow originally registered 180 source pages under cluster-plus-W-id stems such as `source--middle-east-energy-intake-w-225-2026-04-21`, and even the first manual repair still used Korean title slugs instead of the older corpus convention of human-readable English summary stems. Those source identities leaked into synthesis evidence, query pages, and registry exports, so the corpus started reading like a batch ledger instead of a reusable source graph.
observations.why_it_matters: The older corpus relies on concise English summary source identities as the readable default. Once source stems become batch ids or raw title slugs, every downstream surface has to compensate for a naming problem that should have been solved at registration time.
observations.automation_candidate: Require a human-curated English summary slug before source registration closeout. The workflow now includes a reusable slug-manifest scaffold/validate helper and lint/preflight enforcement that fails on registry-first or noncanonical source stems.
observations.suggested_followup: Use `ops/scripts/source_slug_curation.py scaffold` plus a reviewed manifest before future batch source writes, then keep the registry/frontmatter/source-stem parity checks active so future batches cannot close with cluster-plus-W-id or non-ASCII source identities.

observations.problem: Newly promoted synthesis pages use a batch-promotion template centered on routing rationale (`이 묶음이 새로 더하는 것`, `source 신호`, `라우팅 기준`, `이 route를 다시 쓰는 법`) instead of the prose shape used by older mature syntheses. The result is structurally valid but stylistically divergent.
observations.why_it_matters: A synthesis page should read like a stable knowledge object, not like a promotion memo. If new pages keep a different shape, the corpus drifts into two synthesis dialects that are both valid to the runtime but uneven to readers.
observations.automation_candidate: Split promotion-time justification from reader-facing synthesis output. The generator can keep batch-decision trail artifacts in run-local/query surfaces while emitting synthesis pages through a shared mature-synthesis template with the same narrative rhythm as older pages.
observations.suggested_followup: Updated on 2026-04-22 KST: the promotion manifest/runtime now require `synthesis.bridge_source_stems` plus a non-empty `integration_note`, so newly promoted synthesis pages render older bridge evidence and continuity prose directly into the page instead of keeping same-day intake context isolated. Keep the template-drift and follow-up-split review candidates active for future batch closeout.
